auth_notes: Requires custom header configuration in the HTTP Request node. Internal systems often use static API keys or JWTs generated by an internal auth service.
rate_limits: Defined by internal server configuration; typically lacks the sophisticated throttling of public SaaS APIs.
gotchas: Internal APIs often lack comprehensive documentation or OpenAPI/Swagger specs
Network connectivity issues if n8n is cloud-hosted and the accounting system is on-premise (requires VPN/SSH Tunnel)
Data formats may be non-standard (e.g., XML or legacy JSON structures)
client_must_provide: Database schema or API endpoint list
VPN or Firewall access for n8n IP addresses
research_notes: As this is a custom/internal system, the HTTP Request node is the primary tool. You will need to manually define the JSON body for POST/PUT requests and use the 'Split Out' node to handle arrays of accounting data. If the system supports webhooks, a 'Webhook' node can be used as a trigger, but the internal system must be configured to point to the n8n production URL.

effort_recommendation.rationale: As this is a custom/internal system, the HTTP Request node is the primary tool. You will need to manually define the JSON body for POST/PUT requests and use the 'Split Out' node to handle arrays of accounting data. If the system supports webhooks, a 'Webhook' node can be used as a trigger, but the internal system must be configured to point to the n8n production URL.
